items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José City Council reviewed status reports on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan, youth empowerment programs, and the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These updates outline the city's strategy for improving services and facilities for young people and library users.

items.headline: Project at 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a development project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled for a later date to be determined. Residents who wanted to weigh in on this project should watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should watch for follow-up information about what this construction project entails and where it will be located in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City awards contract for Bascom Avenue traffic signal project
items.summary: The council approved a third amendment to extend the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project with consultant DKS Associates, addressing safety at the intersection of a major avenue and railroad tracks. The project aims to improve traffic flow and safety in that corridor.

items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state arts and culture grant
items.summary: San José secured a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza, expanding cultural programming and educational opportunities in the city.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including edits to how projects will be scored and prioritized. This plan affects which biking and pedestrian infrastructure improvements get built in the city.

items.headline: Council vacancy process being changed
items.summary: The city council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled and reduce something (likely the timeline or number of steps). This could affect how quickly new council members are appointed when seats open up.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a major $8.8M senior housing development with 65 units at 678 E St John St. This addresses San José's significant shortage of affordable senior housing options.
